skill.name: gsap-animations
skill.description: Guidance for creating accessible, performant GSAP animations with ScrollTrigger, WordPress enqueue examples, responsive behavior, and visual QA support.
skill.summary: Build accessible and performant GSAP animations for web and WordPress projects.
skill.author: CrazySwami

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 85 potential issues, including command execution, weak cryptography, screenshot upload, system reconnaissance, and hardcoded URLs. AI review found the command, crypto, screenshot, and reconnaissance detections are false positives from markdown examples and animation terminology; only external CDN and documentation URLs remain as a low-risk publication concern.

low_findings.title: External command detections are markdown false positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections are caused by inline markdown terms and fenced code examples. No shell execution instruction, subprocess API, or command injection flow was found in the skill text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside markdown documentation and language fences. Targeted review found no executable shell commands or command dispatch APIs.

low_findings.title: External URLs require normal dependency caution
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to cdnjs, GreenSock documentation, forums, and web.dev resources. They can cause network loading when used, but no secret upload or unauthorized request behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible CDN and documentation references consistent with the GSAP topic. They are external dependencies, but the context does not show exfiltration or covert networking.

low_findings.title: Screenshot and weak crypto detections are semantic false positives
low_findings.description: The screenshot finding refers to local visual QA guidance, not upload behavior. The weak cryptography detections occur in ordinary animation or wording contexts, not cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines discuss animation screenshots, desktop animations, skipping animations, and cleanup. No hashing, encryption, or upload endpoint appears in these contexts.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance detections are wording false positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain animation guidance about layout properties, ScrollTrigger mistakes, and layout thrashing. No host enumeration, identity checks, or environment inspection was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact contexts are web animation recommendations and checklist text. They do not include reconnaissance commands, system APIs, or collection of host information.

content.value_statement: Teams need animations that feel polished without hurting performance or accessibility. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical GSAP patterns for ScrollTrigger, WordPress, responsive behavior, and testing.
actual_capabilities: Guides GSAP and ScrollTrigger setup with CDN and WordPress enqueue examples.
Provides fade, stagger, parallax, text reveal, mask reveal, and hero timeline patterns.
Shows accessibility handling for reduced motion and no-JavaScript content visibility.
Documents responsive matchMedia use, resize refresh, and image-load refresh handling.
Includes debugging, cleanup, launch checklist, and Playwright screenshot timing guidance.
limitations: Does not include runnable package scripts, automated tests, or installed dependencies.
CDN examples depend on third-party availability and site content security policy settings.
Some GSAP plugins may require the correct GreenSock license for production use.
Does not replace manual testing on target browsers, devices, and page templates.

use_cases.title: Add Scroll Effects to a Marketing Page
use_cases.description: Create entrance, parallax, and hero animations while preserving readable content before JavaScript loads.

use_cases.title: Review WordPress Animation Setup
use_cases.description: Check enqueue order, ScrollTrigger registration, reduced motion handling, and launch checklist items.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Visual Regression Tests
use_cases.description: Make animation states predictable before screenshots and reduce flaky visual QA results.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Reveal
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this GSAP animation skill to plan a simple fade-in-on-scroll pattern for my page section. Include accessibility and fallback considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Implement WordPress GSAP Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add GSAP and ScrollTrigger to my WordPress theme. Review enqueue order, dependencies, and where the animation file should load.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Animations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my current GSAP animations for performance, reduced motion support, ScrollTrigger cleanup, and responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Testable Animation System
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a reusable GSAP animation system with data attributes, cleanup methods, responsive rules, and visual QA support.

output_examples.input: Create scroll-based animation guidance for a services grid.
output_examples.output: A structured implementation plan covering staggered card reveals, ScrollTrigger start points, reduced motion behavior, and launch checklist items.

output_examples.input: Review a WordPress theme animation setup.
output_examples.output: A concise audit of script loading, GSAP dependencies, CDN risks, fallback visibility, and mobile performance concerns.

output_examples.input: Prepare animations for visual regression testing.
output_examples.output: A testing workflow that completes or skips animations before screenshots and checks multiple viewport sizes.

best_practices: Animate transform and opacity when possible to reduce layout and paint work.
Respect reduced motion preferences and keep content visible without JavaScript.
Refresh ScrollTrigger after images, resize events, or dynamic content changes.
anti_patterns: Do not hide essential content until an animation script runs successfully.
Do not leave ScrollTrigger debug markers enabled in production.
Do not animate layout-heavy properties such as width, height, top, left, margin, or padding.

faq.question: Does this skill install GSAP automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and examples for adding GSAP to web or WordPress projects.

faq.question: Can it help with WordPress themes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes WordPress enqueue guidance for GSAP core, ScrollTrigger, and theme animation files.

faq.question: Does it cover accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes reduced motion support, visible fallback content, and avoiding critical content hidden by animations.

faq.question: Can it improve animation performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends transform and opacity animation, batching, lazy initialization, cleanup, and refresh timing.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes how to prepare animation states for Playwright screenshots and visual QA workflows.

faq.question: Are external resources required?
faq.answer: The examples reference CDN and documentation URLs. Projects can replace CDN loading with local or bundled GSAP assets.
